meta.title: mem9 - Persistent Memory for TiDB OpenClaw
meta.description: mem9 adds persistent memory storage for OpenClaw agents with searchable recall across sessions, devices, and agents. Hosted or self-hosted.

props.headline: Persistent memory for OpenClaw
props.subheadline: OpenClaw agents lose context between sessions. mem9 adds a persistent memory layer for storage, search, and shared recall across sessions, devices, and agents.

items.title: Persistent across sessions
items.description: Memory survives restarts, reconnects, and machine switches.

items.title: Searchable by default
items.description: Start with keyword retrieval, then add embeddings for hybrid search.

items.title: Shared across agents
items.description: Keep useful context available to multiple agents and tools.

items.title: Hosted or self-hosted
items.description: Use mem9.ai or run it on your own infrastructure.

props.title: Everything needed for persistent agent memory

items.title: Durable memory storage
items.description: Write memory once and keep it available across sessions, machines, and workflows. No fragile local-only state.

items.title: Keyword search first
items.description: Useful retrieval should work immediately. mem9 starts with keyword search out of the box.

items.title: Hybrid retrieval when needed
items.description: Add embeddings later and mem9 can support keyword + vector retrieval without forcing a separate memory stack.

items.title: Shared memory spaces
items.description: Let multiple agents or tools read from and write to the same memory layer when they need common context.

items.title: Open source runtime
items.description: Apache-2.0 licensed server, plugins, and hooks. Use the hosted service or self-host it.

items.title: Minimal operational setup
items.description: No schema work, no custom sync layer, no separate retrieval pipeline to build before getting started.

tabs.label: 1. Connect OpenClaw
tabs.description: Follow the setup instructions in SKILL.md and point OpenClaw to mem9.

tabs.label: 2. Write memory during use
tabs.description: Your agent stores useful context through the mem9 memory interface.

tabs.label: 3. Retrieve across sessions
tabs.description: mem9 keeps memory durable and searchable so the agent can reuse it later.

tabs.label: 4. Manage centrally
tabs.description: Use the mem9.ai app or API to inspect, search, import, and export memory.

items.title: OpenClaw
items.description: Persistent memory for OpenClaw agents, with recall across sessions and devices.

items.title: Your Memory
items.description: Use the mem9.ai interface to inspect, manage, analyze, import, and export stored memory.

items.title: API clients
items.description: Also works with clients that read from or write to the mem9 API.

props.title: Security and data handling
props.subtitle: mem9 is designed to store and retrieve agent memory with clear operational boundaries.

items.title: Encryption in transit and at rest
items.description: Memory data is protected over the network and while stored.

items.title: Access controls
items.description: Production access is limited to systems and operators that require it.

items.title: Auditability
items.description: Operational actions are logged and observable for review and troubleshooting.

items.title: Isolated service boundaries
items.description: Memory processing is scoped to reduce unnecessary data exposure.

items.title: Durable cloud infrastructure
items.description: The hosted service runs on infrastructure designed for reliability, backup, and steady operation.

items.a: No. mem9 works with any client that can read from or write to the mem9 API. OpenClaw is the primary onboarding path today.
mem9 handles long-lived memory for agents. It stores, retrieves, and shares memory across sessions, devices, and workflows.

items.q: Do I need a separate vector database?
items.a: No. You can start with keyword retrieval. If you add embeddings, mem9 can support hybrid retrieval without requiring a separate DIY memory stack.
Yes. mem9 is open source and self-hostable.
mem9 uses encryption in transit and at rest, access controls, auditability, and scoped service boundaries. Additional security information is available in the security overview.

props.title: Start with persistent memory for OpenClaw
props.subtitle: Install mem9, connect it to OpenClaw, and keep agent memory available across sessions.
